methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
The ePlane Company is a Series B transportation-sector startup founded by Satya Chakravarthy, having raised $14M+ and currently seeking $40–50M in new capital. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies in adjacent transportation segments, including Series A–C stage businesses such as Einride ($1.8B), Harbinger ($500M), and REGENT Craft ($270M). No direct public comparables exist in this frontier segment.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression anchors to the $14M raised proxy and applies a typical 1.9x multiplier for Series B advancement, implying a $27M valuation. Peer-median benchmarking uses three same-sector, same-or-adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $500M and a range of $270M–$1.8B. This method reflects substantial heterogeneity in investor conviction across the sector.

The triangulated range spans $23M (low) to $575M (high) with a median of $263M, reflecting material tension between stage-mechanical progression and peer-based market pricing. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by variance in peer outcomes rather than analytical disagreement—points to the core valuation uncertainty: early-stage transportation technology exhibits high outcome dispersion, and peer selections significantly influence implied value. Key unknowns include product-market validation trajectory, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital efficiency relative to peers.
